deleteDialog.title: Remove customer
deleteDialog.description: Are you sure you want to remove {{name}} from the workspace? This action cannot be undone.

customerImport.title: Import customers via CSV
customerImport.productIdsHint: In the product_ids column, enter the product ID registered in Nory Members (e.g.: prod_4jn235h1343). You can find this ID on the product page or in its URL in the admin panel. To link more than one product to the same customer, separate the IDs with a comma.
customerImport.optionalColumnsValue: first_name, last_name, phone_country_code, phone, city, state, country, document_type, document, external_id
customerImport.schemaSubtitle: All accepted columns for customer CSV import.

product_ids.description: IDs of products registered in Nory Members. Find the ID on the product page or in the product URL in your Nory Members admin. E.g.: prod_4jn235h1343. Separate multiple IDs with a comma.

first_name.description: Customer's first name (optional if name is filled)

last_name.description: Customer's last name (optional if name is filled)

phone_country_code.description: Phone country code. E.g.: 55. Nory Members normalizes automatically (removes +, parentheses and spaces).

phone.description: Phone number. E.g.: 11999999999. Nory Members normalizes automatically (keeps only digits).

state.description: State with exactly 2 letters. E.g.: SP. Values that are not 2 letters will be ignored.

document_type.description: Document type. E.g.: CPF, CNPJ, PASSPORT, DNI, ID, RUT, EIN, VAT.

document.description: Document number or code. Nory Members normalizes automatically (keeps only letters and digits).

external_id.description: Customer/contact ID within your own system, CRM, or any external platform you want to reference, if applicable.

customerImport.noEmailSent: No emails will be sent to contacts during the import.
customerImport.uploadCsv: Click to select or drag the file
customerImport.uploadHint: CSV format, up to {{max}} rows, UTF-8 encoding
customerImport.duplicateWarning: {{count}} email(s) already exist in your database.
customerImport.duplicateNote: They will be merged (empty fields filled from CSV, existing data kept).
customerImport.newCustomers: {{count}} are new and will be created.
customerImport.unresolvedWarning: {{rowCount}} row(s) with product ID(s) not found. Total of {{count}} occurrence(s). If you proceed, these product IDs from your spreadsheet will be ignored and the customer-product link will not be made for these rows, but the customers will still be imported normally.
customerImport.errorText: {{count}} row(s) with errors will be ignored.

progress.doNotClose: Do not close this page during the import

result.title: Import completed successfully
result.skippedDesc: These emails were already registered. Existing data was kept (empty fields were filled from CSV).

links.title: Useful links
links.subtitle: Add external links such as articles, tools or supplementary references. Maximum 10 per lesson.
links.urlDescription: The actual URL/address the user will be directed to. Great for including utm or params.
links.displayUrlDescription: Friendly URL/address shown to your customers. Useful for hiding utm, params or long links.
links.limitReached: Limit of 10 links per lesson reached.

image.invalidFormat: Unsupported format. Use JPG, PNG, GIF or WebP.

mode.richDescription: Format with headings, lists, images and smart blocks.
mode.htmlDescription: Paste ready-made HTML: useful for iframes, forms and widgets.
mode.activeHint: Only the selected mode is shown in the lesson. The other one stays saved but is invisible to students.

htmlView.empty: Nothing to preview yet. Paste HTML in the other tab.

mode.limitationScripts: Scripts and inline handlers (onclick, onerror, etc.) are removed automatically for security.
mode.limitationStyles: Inline styles (font-family, font-size, color) are stripped — typography follows the platform design.
mode.limitationImages: Images must be hosted at public HTTPS URLs. Direct upload only works in the text editor.
mode.limitationForms: Forms and interactive elements may not work as expected — prefer iframe embeds.

creators.subtitle: Hundreds of creators and companies already trust Nory Members for their communities.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How do I create my first course?
faq.a1: It's simple! After creating your account, go to the dashboard, click 'New Course' and use our drag-and-drop editor to build modules and lessons. You can add videos, text, audio and files to each lesson.
faq.q2: Who has access to the courses?
faq.a2: You have full control over access. You can create free, paid, or exclusive courses for customers on specific plans. Each student only sees what they have permission for.
faq.q3: What platforms does it work on?
faq.a3: Your students can access courses via web browser on any device — computer, tablet or phone. The interface is 100% responsive.
faq.q4: Can I sell my courses through the platform?
faq.a4: Yes! You can create sales pages, set prices (one-time or subscription), apply discount coupons and receive payments directly.
faq.q5: Is there a limit on students or courses?
faq.a5: No! You can create as many courses as you want and enroll as many students as you need, with no artificial limits.

vimeo.connectDescription: Connect your Vimeo video library to select videos directly in your lessons.
vimeo.guideStep1: Go to the Vimeo apps dashboard:
vimeo.guideStep2: Click your app (or create a new one by clicking "Create an app").
vimeo.guideStep3: Scroll to "Personal Access Tokens". Under "Scopes", check: Public, Private, and Video Files.
vimeo.guideStep4: Click "Generate" and copy the token (it is shown only once).
vimeo.guideStep5: Paste the token in the field below and click "Connect Vimeo".

vimeo.disconnectDescription: Removes the integration. Lessons already using Vimeo videos will keep working.

pandavideo.guideStep3: Paste the key below and click "Connect Panda Video".

guides.title: Guides
guides.listingDescription: Learn to use Nory Members like a pro. Practical guides, clear explanations, and visual examples to get the most out of the platform.

customerPortal.title: Understanding the Customer Portal
customerPortal.intro: The Customer Portal is the exclusive area where your students and buyers access the products they purchased. Here you'll understand everything about how it works, what your customers see, and how to get the most out of this feature.

whatIs.title: What is the Customer Portal?
whatIs.description: The Portal is a secure and personalized environment branded with your business identity. It's where your customers access courses, download files, track orders, and manage their data. Each workspace on Nory Members has its own portal, accessible via a unique URL.
whatIs.feature1Desc: Your customers see all the products they've purchased in one place, organized in visual cards.
whatIs.feature2Desc: Direct access to courses with video player, lesson progress, and organized modules.
whatIs.feature3Desc: File-type products are available for download with secure, temporary links.
whatIs.feature4Desc: Customers can edit their profile and check their complete purchase and payment history.

howToAccess.title: How does the customer access the portal?
howToAccess.description: Portal access is simple and secure. The customer doesn't need to create a password — they receive a magic link via email.
howToAccess.step1Desc: The customer visits your portal URL (e.g., seudominio.com/your-school/login) or clicks the link you shared.
howToAccess.step2Desc: They enter the email used for the purchase. The system sends a direct access link to their inbox.
howToAccess.step3Desc: By clicking the link received via email, the customer is automatically authenticated and redirected to the portal.
howToAccess.step4Desc: Once authenticated, the customer sees all purchased products and can access courses, download files, and more.
howToAccess.tip: The first time a customer accesses via magic link, they can set a password for future logins. This is optional — the magic link always works.

navigation.title: Navigating the portal
navigation.description: The portal has a sidebar with 4 main sections. Here's how each one works:
navigation.homeDesc: Gallery with all customer products. Visual cards with cover image, name, and course progress.
navigation.profileDesc: Form to edit name, phone, city, state, and country. Email cannot be changed.
navigation.ordersDesc: Table with complete purchase history: product, amount, payment status, and date.
navigation.productsDesc: List of purchased products with type (Courses or Files) and direct content access.

productsAccess.title: Products & access
productsAccess.description: The portal displays customer products as visual cards. Purchased products are active, products without access appear faded with a lock icon.
productsAccess.hasAccessDesc: The customer can click to access the course or download files. For courses, the progress bar shows how much has been watched.
productsAccess.noAccessDesc: Appears faded with a lock icon. The customer can see the product exists but can't access the content. Great for sparking interest in new products.

orders.title: Order history
orders.description: In the Orders section, the customer sees a table with all their purchases, including payment status with the same colored badges used in the admin panel.

filesCourses.title: Files & courses
filesCourses.description: Depending on the product type, the customer has access to different features within the portal.
filesCourses.tip: Download links are temporary (expire in 1 hour) and generated on demand for security. The customer can generate a new link whenever needed.

bestPractices.title: Tips to get the most out of the portal
bestPractices.tip1Desc: Set up your logo, colors, and login page background image in Design settings. Your portal will match your brand.
bestPractices.tip2Desc: Send the portal URL (seudominio.com/your-school) to your customers via email, WhatsApp, or social media. They access directly, no searching needed.
bestPractices.tip3Desc: Courses with well-structured modules make student navigation easier and increase completion rates. Progress is saved automatically.
bestPractices.tip4Desc: Products with cover images attract much more attention in the portal gallery. Use high-quality images to improve the visual experience.

orderStatuses.title: Understanding Order Statuses
orderStatuses.intro: Each order in Nory Members goes through different phases, represented by a status. Understanding what each one means helps you track your sales, quickly identify issues, and know when to take action.

pending.label: Pending
pending.description: The order was created, but payment has not been confirmed yet. This is the initial state of every transaction.
pending.when: As soon as the customer starts checkout or when a boleto/Pix payment hasn't been identified yet.

approved.label: Approved
approved.description: Payment has been successfully confirmed! Content access is automatically granted to the student.
approved.when: When the payment gateway confirms the amount was received (card approved, Pix identified, boleto cleared).

completed.label: Completed
completed.description: The order has been fully processed and finalized. For subscriptions, it indicates all billing cycles have been paid. Access remains active.
completed.when: After full payment processing or when a subscription completes all billing cycles.

refunded.label: Refunded
refunded.description: The amount was returned to the customer. Content access is automatically revoked.
refunded.when: When you process a refund through the gateway or when the customer requests a return within the deadline.

cancelled.label: Cancelled
cancelled.description: The order was cancelled before completion. Access is automatically revoked.
cancelled.when: When payment expires (expired boleto, unpaid Pix), when there's a card failure, or manual cancellation.

disputed.label: Disputed
disputed.description: The customer opened a dispute with the payment gateway. This is a warning signal, but the student's access is maintained while the dispute is unresolved.
disputed.when: When the customer disputes the charge on their card or opens a complaint with the gateway.

chargeback.label: Chargeback
chargeback.description: The bank or card operator ruled in favor of the customer and reversed the payment. Access is automatically revoked — this is a terminal state.
chargeback.when: When the bank finalizes the dispute in the customer's favor and reverses the amount from your account.

orderStatuses.summaryTableIntro: See all statuses at a glance, with their meaning and impact on student access:
orderStatuses.flowIntro: In the most common scenario, an order follows this path:
orderStatuses.flowHappyPath: This is the ideal flow (happy path) — from pending payment to completion.
orderStatuses.flowTip: Orders with Disputed or Chargeback status follow a different path. Check the specific guide to understand each scenario.
orderStatuses.mockupTitle: How it looks on your orders screen
orderStatuses.mockupIntro: Here's an example of how the orders table appears in the Nory Members admin panel, with colored badges for each status:
orderStatuses.mockupCaption: Illustrative example — the data above is fictional.

disputedVsChargeback.title: Disputed vs Chargeback: What's the Difference?
disputedVsChargeback.intro: Two statuses that often cause confusion, but have very different impacts on your business and your students' access. Understanding the difference is essential to know how to react in each situation.

disputed.title: Disputed
disputed.description: A dispute is an intermediate state — it means the customer opened a claim, but nothing has been decided yet. Think of it as a "warning signal" that needs your attention.
disputed.when: The customer opens a dispute/complaint with the payment gateway. For example: Hotmart sends a PURCHASE_PROTEST event, Kiwify signals an open dispute.
disputed.whatHappens: The system marks the order as "Disputed" but does NOT revoke student access and does NOT alter the amount. It's just a status change so you know there's an ongoing dispute.
disputed.accessDetail: The student continues with full access to the content. Nory Members takes no automatic action on access while the dispute is unresolved.
disputed.nextSteps: The dispute can be resolved in the seller's favor (returns to Approved) or evolve into a chargeback/refund. Monitor through your payment gateway.

chargeback.title: Chargeback
chargeback.description: Chargeback is a terminal state — the bank has already ruled in the customer's favor and reversed the payment. The money has left your account and student access is automatically revoked.
chargeback.when: The bank or card operator finalizes the dispute in the customer's favor and reverses the amount. Gateways send events like PURCHASE_CHARGEBACK (Hotmart), chargedback (Kiwify), or SALE_CHARGEBACK (Kirvano).
chargeback.whatHappens: The system marks the order as "Chargeback" and automatically revokes student access — the same logic applied in refunds and cancellations.
chargeback.accessDetail: Student access is automatically removed. The system treats chargeback the same way as a refund: revokes all access linked to the order.

table.disputedBehavior: Only updates the order status — doesn't touch access or amount
table.chargebackBehavior: Updates the status and automatically revokes student access

disputedVsChargeback.tldrDisputed: Warning signal — the customer disputed, but it can still be resolved in your favor. Student access remains active.
disputedVsChargeback.tldrChargeback: Confirmed loss — the bank reversed the payment and student access was automatically removed.

intro.title: Nory Members API
intro.description: Complete documentation of the Nory Members REST API — B2B2C membership and members area. All requests use the POST method, accept JSON and return JSON.
intro.authDescription: All requests require a token in the Authorization header.
intro.jwtDescription: Generated after login. For actions that require authenticated user context.
intro.errorDescription: When something goes wrong, the API returns a standardized error object with message, code, and HTTP status.

dialog.description: Describe what your course is about and AI will suggest a title and description.
dialog.promptPlaceholder: E.g.: Excel course for beginners, focused on pivot tables and basic formulas
